purpose.summary: Machine-readable corpus and bounded tool interface for EML — a deterministic semantic-overlay layer that compresses high-frequency program intent into symbols and transpiles, rule-based and reversibly, to Python.

specs.role: self-contained AI-semantic spec: status vocabulary, twelve-loop taxonomy, bug/repair/criticality models

examples.title: Arithmetic & augmented assign
examples.description: Demonstrates the two-stage `^+` rule (declare then augment) and `^*` / `^0`.

examples.title: Summation (Σ) over an inclusive range
examples.description: The canonical EML demo: an algebraic-sum intent compressed into one symbol.

examples.title: Cold / hot functions
examples.description: `@cold` pure logic becomes cacheable (`@functools.cache`, auto-imported); `@hot` is a marker only. Function definitions and `@cold` round-trip. `@hot` has one documented cosmetic exception.

examples.title: Closures (nested functions)
examples.description: A nested `def` captures the enclosing parameter `n` (lexical closure).

examples.title: Recursion
examples.description: Self-recursive factorial via the ternary `?:`. `loopKind: recursive` (deterministic; not statically proven terminating).

examples.title: MVP proof: a second real, recognizable program ported to EML
examples.description: `number_guessing_game.eml` is the second "does EML actually work for something real" test (see [`examples/mvp-tic-tac-toe/`](../mvp-tic-tac-toe/) for the first) — a genuine, independently-authored small program expressed end to end in EML: transpiled to Python, executed, and verified against real Python via the interpreter equivalence gate.

examples.title: MVP proof: a real, recognizable program ported to EML
examples.description: `tic_tac_toe.eml` is a "does EML actually work for something real" test — not a new-language-feature regression fixture like the other `examples/phaseN-*` directories, but a demonstration that a genuine, independently-authored small program can be expressed end to end in EML: transpiled to Python, executed, and verified against real Python via the interpreter equivalence gate.

examples.title: Case corpus: a self-authored class-based todo-list manager
examples.description: `todo_list_manager.eml` is the third case in a self-authored batch (see [`examples/unit-temperature-converter/`](../unit-temperature-converter/) and [`examples/word-frequency-counter/`](../word-frequency-counter/) for the other two) — part of growing the EML case corpus toward AI-native training scale, not a port of an existing project.

examples.title: Case corpus: a self-authored unit-conversion program
examples.description: `temperature_converter.eml` is the first case in a self-authored batch (see [`examples/word-frequency-counter/`](../word-frequency-counter/) and [`examples/todo-list-manager/`](../todo-list-manager/) for the other two) — part of growing the EML case corpus toward AI-native training scale, not a port of an existing project.

examples.title: Case corpus: a self-authored word-frequency counter
examples.description: `word_frequency_counter.eml` is the second case in a self-authored batch (see [`examples/unit-temperature-converter/`](../unit-temperature-converter/) and [`examples/todo-list-manager/`](../todo-list-manager/) for the other two) — part of growing the EML case corpus toward AI-native training scale, not a port of an existing project.

agent_guidance: Fetch this manifest first, then follow reading_order.
Prefer structured schemas (AST/trace/error) over UI text.
ASCII canonical form is normative; Unicode (Σ ∈ ⇒ ²) is a display projection.
Do not infer unsupported syntax; the EBNF grammar is the authority.
Call tools only with bounded EML input (<= 20000 chars).

Function definitions, class, @cold, and matrices (<M>/np.array) are fully round-trippable. Only @hot's comment-only marker (cosmetic, not functional), @temporal_loop, and async/await remain forward-only (not round-trippable).
